revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (en:Amoghasiddhi); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text: {{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:29, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-25, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1198627240 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:29, 25 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start| {{WikiProject Buddhism|importance=mid}} {{WikiProject China|importance=Mid}} }} ==Name/meaning== Sometimes it is Amoghasiddhi and sometimes it is Amoghasiddha. As far as I understand one is female form and one is male form, grammatically. :Austerlitz -- [[Special:Contributions/88.72.18.141|88.72.18.141]] ([[User talk:88.72.18.141|talk]]) 07:47, 9 October 2008 (UTC) :* [[Image:Arrowred.png|15px]] Etymologically, this is a compound of [[Sanskrit language|Sanskrit]] अमोघ ''amogha'', "not empty, not in vain" + [[wikt:सिद्धि|सिद्धि]] ''[[siddhi]]'', "success, attainment, perfection". The ''amogha'' part shows up in other names, such as [[Amoghavajra]] or [[Avalokiteśvara#Tibetan_account|Amoghapāśa]]. :: See also the related entries at the Spoken Sanskrit website for [http://spokensanskrit.de/index.php?script=HK&beginning=0+&tinput=amogha&trans=Translate&direction=AU ''amogha''] and [http://spokensanskrit.de/index.php?script=HK&beginning=0+&tinput=siddhi&trans=Translate&direction=AU ''siddhi'']. :: -- [[User:Eirikr|Eiríkr Útlendi]] │ ''[[User talk:Eirikr|Tala við mig]]'' 19:21, 24 July 2013 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:29, 25 February 2026 (UTC) ==Others== The Great Buddha did manifest to me, via a turtle at petco.[[User:Lazzara399|Lazzara399]] ([[User talk:Lazzara399|talk]]) 00:57, 2 July 2010 (UTC) Hi guys, wanted to see if anyone had any objections about adding a section on Amogasiddhi in Tibetan buddhism, talking about his mantra, Yidams in his family and his ruling over Wrathful action. Let me know what you guys think, thanks. [[Special:Contributions/2600:1700:DB70:CA0:6D32:5244:958:3C81|2600:1700:DB70:CA0:6D32:5244:958:3C81]] ([[User talk:2600:1700:DB70:CA0:6D32:5244:958:3C81|talk]]) 15:16, 16 July 2020 (UTC) :Om amoghashiddi hung [[Special:Contributions/202.51.76.81|202.51.76.81]] ([[User talk:202.51.76.81|talk]]) 02:35, 12 August 2023 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:29, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == External links modified == Hello fellow Wikipedians, I have just modified {{plural:1|one external link|1 external links}} on [[Amoghasiddhi]]. Please take a moment to review [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?diff=prev&oldid=743893164 my edit]. If you have any questions, or need the bot to ignore the links, or the page altogether, please visit [[User:Cyberpower678/FaQs#InternetArchiveBot|this simple FaQ]] for additional information. I made the following changes: *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20090121225526/http://buddhism.kalachakranet.org/images/double_dorje.gif to http://buddhism.kalachakranet.org/images/double_dorje.gif When you have finished reviewing my changes, please set the ''checked'' parameter below to '''true''' or '''failed''' to let others know (documentation at {{tlx|Sourcecheck}}). {{sourcecheck|checked=false}} Cheers.—[[User:InternetArchiveBot|'''InternetArchiveBot''']] ([[User talk:InternetArchiveBot|Report bug]]) 21:43, 11 October 2016 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:29, 25 February 2026 (UTC)

revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (en:Amoghasiddhi); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text: {{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:30, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:29, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-25, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1198627240 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:30, 25 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start| {{WikiProject Buddhism|importance=mid}} {{WikiProject China|importance=Mid}} }} ==Name/meaning== Sometimes it is Amoghasiddhi and sometimes it is Amoghasiddha. As far as I understand one is female form and one is male form, grammatically. :Austerlitz -- [[Special:Contributions/88.72.18.141|88.72.18.141]] ([[User talk:88.72.18.141|talk]]) 07:47, 9 October 2008 (UTC) :* [[Image:Arrowred.png|15px]] Etymologically, this is a compound of [[Sanskrit language|Sanskrit]] अमोघ ''amogha'', "not empty, not in vain" + [[wikt:सिद्धि|सिद्धि]] ''[[siddhi]]'', "success, attainment, perfection". The ''amogha'' part shows up in other names, such as [[Amoghavajra]] or [[Avalokiteśvara#Tibetan_account|Amoghapāśa]]. :: See also the related entries at the Spoken Sanskrit website for [http://spokensanskrit.de/index.php?script=HK&beginning=0+&tinput=amogha&trans=Translate&direction=AU ''amogha''] and [http://spokensanskrit.de/index.php?script=HK&beginning=0+&tinput=siddhi&trans=Translate&direction=AU ''siddhi'']. :: -- [[User:Eirikr|Eiríkr Útlendi]] │ ''[[User talk:Eirikr|Tala við mig]]'' 19:21, 24 July 2013 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:30, 25 February 2026 (UTC) ==Others== The Great Buddha did manifest to me, via a turtle at petco.[[User:Lazzara399|Lazzara399]] ([[User talk:Lazzara399|talk]]) 00:57, 2 July 2010 (UTC) Hi guys, wanted to see if anyone had any objections about adding a section on Amogasiddhi in Tibetan buddhism, talking about his mantra, Yidams in his family and his ruling over Wrathful action. Let me know what you guys think, thanks. [[Special:Contributions/2600:1700:DB70:CA0:6D32:5244:958:3C81|2600:1700:DB70:CA0:6D32:5244:958:3C81]] ([[User talk:2600:1700:DB70:CA0:6D32:5244:958:3C81|talk]]) 15:16, 16 July 2020 (UTC) :Om amoghashiddi hung [[Special:Contributions/202.51.76.81|202.51.76.81]] ([[User talk:202.51.76.81|talk]]) 02:35, 12 August 2023 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:30, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == External links modified == Hello fellow Wikipedians, I have just modified {{plural:1|one external link|1 external links}} on [[Amoghasiddhi]]. Please take a moment to review [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?diff=prev&oldid=743893164 my edit]. If you have any questions, or need the bot to ignore the links, or the page altogether, please visit [[User:Cyberpower678/FaQs#InternetArchiveBot|this simple FaQ]] for additional information. I made the following changes: *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20090121225526/http://buddhism.kalachakranet.org/images/double_dorje.gif to http://buddhism.kalachakranet.org/images/double_dorje.gif When you have finished reviewing my changes, please set the ''checked'' parameter below to '''true''' or '''failed''' to let others know (documentation at {{tlx|Sourcecheck}}). {{sourcecheck|checked=false}} Cheers.—[[User:InternetArchiveBot|'''InternetArchiveBot''']] ([[User talk:InternetArchiveBot|Report bug]]) 21:43, 11 October 2016 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:30, 25 February 2026 (UTC)

revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (en:Amoghasiddhi); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text: {{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:30, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:29, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-25, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1198627240 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 25 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start| {{WikiProject Buddhism|importance=mid}} {{WikiProject China|importance=Mid}} }} ==Name/meaning== Sometimes it is Amoghasiddhi and sometimes it is Amoghasiddha. As far as I understand one is female form and one is male form, grammatically. :Austerlitz -- [[Special:Contributions/88.72.18.141|88.72.18.141]] ([[User talk:88.72.18.141|talk]]) 07:47, 9 October 2008 (UTC) :* [[Image:Arrowred.png|15px]] Etymologically, this is a compound of [[Sanskrit language|Sanskrit]] अमोघ ''amogha'', "not empty, not in vain" + [[wikt:सिद्धि|सिद्धि]] ''[[siddhi]]'', "success, attainment, perfection". The ''amogha'' part shows up in other names, such as [[Amoghavajra]] or [[Avalokiteśvara#Tibetan_account|Amoghapāśa]]. :: See also the related entries at the Spoken Sanskrit website for [http://spokensanskrit.de/index.php?script=HK&beginning=0+&tinput=amogha&trans=Translate&direction=AU ''amogha''] and [http://spokensanskrit.de/index.php?script=HK&beginning=0+&tinput=siddhi&trans=Translate&direction=AU ''siddhi'']. :: -- [[User:Eirikr|Eiríkr Útlendi]] │ ''[[User talk:Eirikr|Tala við mig]]'' 19:21, 24 July 2013 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 25 February 2026 (UTC) ==Others== The Great Buddha did manifest to me, via a turtle at petco.[[User:Lazzara399|Lazzara399]] ([[User talk:Lazzara399|talk]]) 00:57, 2 July 2010 (UTC) Hi guys, wanted to see if anyone had any objections about adding a section on Amogasiddhi in Tibetan buddhism, talking about his mantra, Yidams in his family and his ruling over Wrathful action. Let me know what you guys think, thanks. As far as I understand one is female form and one is male form, grammatically. :Austerlitz -- [[Special:Contributions/88.72.18.141|88.72.18.141]] ([[User talk:88.72.18.141|talk]]) 07:47, 9 October 2008 (UTC) :* [[Image:Arrowred.png|15px]] Etymologically, this is a compound of [[Sanskrit language|Sanskrit]] अमोघ ''amogha'', "not empty, not in vain" + [[wikt:सिद्धि|सिद्धि]] ''[[siddhi]]'', "success, attainment, perfection". The ''amogha'' part shows up in other names, such as [[Amoghavajra]] or [[Avalokiteśvara#Tibetan_account|Amoghapāśa]]. :: See also the related entries at the Spoken Sanskrit website for [http://spokensanskrit.de/index.php?script=HK&beginning=0+&tinput=amogha&trans=Translate&direction=AU ''amogha''] and [http://spokensanskrit.de/index.php?script=HK&beginning=0+&tinput=siddhi&trans=Translate&direction=AU ''siddhi'']. :: -- [[User:Eirikr|Eiríkr Útlendi]] │ ''[[User talk:Eirikr|Tala við mig]]'' 19:21, 24 July 2013 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:46, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==Others== The Great Buddha did manifest to me, via a turtle at petco.[[User:Lazzara399|Lazzara399]] ([[User talk:Lazzara399|talk]]) 00:57, 2 July 2010 (UTC) Hi guys, wanted to see if anyone had any objections about adding a section on Amogasiddhi in Tibetan buddhism, talking about his mantra, Yidams in his family and his ruling over Wrathful action. Let me know what you guys think, thanks. [[Special:Contributions/2600:1700:DB70:CA0:6D32:5244:958:3C81|2600:1700:DB70:CA0:6D32:5244:958:3C81]] ([[User talk:2600:1700:DB70:CA0:6D32:5244:958:3C81|talk]]) 15:16, 16 July 2020 (UTC) :Om amoghashiddi hung [[Special:Contributions/202.51.76.81|202.51.76.81]] ([[User talk:202.51.76.81|talk]]) 02:35, 12 August 2023 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:46, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == External links modified == Hello fellow Wikipedians, I have just modified {{plural:1|one external link|1 external links}} on [[Amoghasiddhi]]. revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] previous contributors were [[User:en>LicenceToCrenellate]], [[User:Immanuelle]], [[User:EmmaBot]], see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Amoghasiddhi here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:46, 26 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:30, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:29, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1198627240 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:46, 26 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start| {{WikiProject Buddhism|importance=mid}} {{WikiProject China|importance=Mid}} }} ==Name/meaning== Sometimes it is Amoghasiddhi and sometimes it is Amoghasiddha. As far as I understand one is female form and one is male form, grammatically. :Austerlitz -- [[Special:Contributions/88.72.18.141|88.72.18.141]] ([[User talk:88.72.18.141|talk]]) 07:47, 9 October 2008 (UTC) :* [[Image:Arrowred.png|15px]] Etymologically, this is a compound of [[Sanskrit language|Sanskrit]] अमोघ ''amogha'', "not empty, not in vain" + [[wikt:सिद्धि|सिद्धि]] ''[[siddhi]]'', "success, attainment, perfection". The ''amogha'' part shows up in other names, such as [[Amoghavajra]] or [[Avalokiteśvara#Tibetan_account|Amoghapāśa]]. :: See also the related entries at the Spoken Sanskrit website for [http://spokensanskrit.de/index.php?script=HK&beginning=0+&tinput=amogha&trans=Translate&direction=AU ''amogha''] and [http://spokensanskrit.de/index.php?script=HK&beginning=0+&tinput=siddhi&trans=Translate&direction=AU ''siddhi'']. :: -- [[User:Eirikr|Eiríkr Útlendi]] │ ''[[User talk:Eirikr|Tala við mig]]'' 19:21, 24 July 2013 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:46, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==Others== The Great Buddha did manifest to me, via a turtle at petco.[[User:Lazzara399|Lazzara399]] ([[User talk:Lazzara399|talk]]) 00:57, 2 July 2010 (UTC) Hi guys, wanted to see if anyone had any objections about adding a section on Amogasiddhi in Tibetan buddhism, talking about his mantra, Yidams in his family and his ruling over Wrathful action. Let me know what you guys think, thanks.
